title: Graceful Timeout Handler
constraints: You must not remove or disable the slow tool
The timeout threshold must be configurable, not hardcoded
The agent must return a user-friendly message on timeout
Normal tool responses that complete in time must still work
hints: Python's signal module or threading.Timer can enforce time limits on synchronous calls
Wrap the tool call in a helper that raises a custom exception after the timeout elapses
Catch the timeout exception and return a friendly fallback string instead of crashing

starterCode.langchain:
import time
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
from langchain_core.tools import tool

@tool
def slow_api_lookup(query: str) -> str:
    """Look up information from an external API."""
    # BUG: This tool takes 30 seconds and the agent has no timeout protection
    time.sleep(30)
    return f"Results for: {query}"

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a helpful assistant. Use tools to answer questions."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
    ("placeholder", "{agent_scratchpad}"),
])

agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, [slow_api_lookup], prompt)
executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=[slow_api_lookup])

# Test: This will hang for 30 seconds with no feedback
result = executor.invoke({"input": "Look up the latest AI news"})
print(result["output"])
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
import time
from agents import Agent, Runner
from agents.tool import function_tool

@function_tool
def slow_api_lookup(query: str) -> str:
    """Look up information from an external API."""
    # BUG: This tool takes 30 seconds and the agent has no timeout protection
    time.sleep(30)
    return f"Results for: {query}"

agent = Agent(
    name="Research Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant. Use tools to answer questions.",
    tools=[slow_api_lookup],
)

# Test: This will hang for 30 seconds with no feedback
result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "Look up the latest AI news")
print(result.final_output)
starterCode.crewai:
import time
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai.tools import tool

@tool
def slow_api_lookup(query: str) -> str:
    """Look up information from an external API."""
    # BUG: This tool takes 30 seconds and the agent has no timeout protection
    time.sleep(30)
    return f"Results for: {query}"

assistant = Agent(
    role="Research Assistant",
    goal="Answer questions using tools",
    backstory="You are a helpful assistant. Use tools to answer questions.",
    tools=[slow_api_lookup],
)

# Test: This will hang for 30 seconds with no feedback
task = Task(
    description="Look up the latest AI news",
    expected_output="Latest AI news results",
    agent=assistant,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[assistant], tasks=[task])
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)

evaluationCriteria.label: Timeout is enforced
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent does not hang indefinitely when a tool takes too long

evaluationCriteria.label: Returns friendly message
evaluationCriteria.description: On timeout, the user receives a helpful message instead of an error or crash

evaluationCriteria.label: Fast tools still work
evaluationCriteria.description: Tools that respond within the timeout window continue to work normally
